sanskrit:
सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।
अहं त्वां सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥
transliteration: sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja | ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Having abandoned all forms of dharma, take refuge in Me alone; I will release you from all evils—do not grieve.
contextual_meaning: Krishna offers Arjuna a single, anxiety-shattering pivot: drop every mental schema (including moral ones) and trust one internalized secure base; the psyche is then cleared of guilt and fear.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic growth moment: ego schemas dissolve into a felt-sense of safe presence; cortisol drops, oxytocin rises, narrative identity shifts from performer to beloved.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out perfectionist who has tried every self-help and spiritual path finally lets go of the checklist and texts a trusted friend 'I can’t anymore—can you just hold me?' and feels, for the first time, unclenched.
anchor_text: Let go of every rule and trust the quiet presence within.
